Question: Using the definition, determine whether the function $$f ( x ) = \{ \left .\begin{matrix}{aa}2 x + 1 & \text{if} x < 1 \\ 2 & \text{if} x = 1 \\ - x + 4 & \text{if} x > 1\end{matrix}\right .$$ is continuous at $$x = 1 .$$ If the function is not continuous at 1, indicate the condition for continuity at a point that fails to hold.
Answer: f is not continuous at 1 because $$f \left(\right. 1 \left.\right) = 2 \neq 3 = \underset{x \rightarrow 1}{\text{lim}} f \left(\right. x \left.\right) .$$

Question: For what values of x is $$f ( x ) = 3 x^{4} - 4 x^{2}$$ continuous?
Answer: $$f ( x )$$ is continuous at every real number.

Question: For $$f ( x ) = \{ \left .\begin{matrix}{aa}x^{2} & \text{if} x \ne 1 \\ 3 & \text{if} x = 1\end{matrix}\right . ,$$ decide whether f is continuous at 1. If f is not continuous at 1, classify the discontinuity as removable, jump, or infinite.
State the interval(s) over which the function $$f ( x ) = \sqrt{x + 3}$$ is continuous.
Show that $$f ( x ) = x^{3} - x^{2} - 3 x + 1$$ has a zero over the interval $$[ 0 , 1 ] .$$
Answer: $$f ( 0 ) = 1 > 0 , f ( 1 ) = -2 < 0 ; f ( x )$$ is continuous over $$[ 0 , 1 ] .$$ It must have a zero on this interval.
